name: Neuquen (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the celestial blue of the Argentine flag and of the Patagonian sky
the snow of the Andean cordillera; the central stripe of the Argentine flag
the pehuen (Araucaria araucana), the sacred tree of the Mapuche-Pehuenche peoples and provincial tree of Neuquen
the eight stars representing the eight points of contact between the province's geography and history
the kultrun and the blood of the original Mapuche-Pehuenche peoples

description: A vertical triband of light blue, white and light blue, charged in the centre with a stylised green pehuen tree above a red eight-pointed star, all surrounded by a circle of eight small gold four-pointed stars.
history: Adopted by provincial law 2495 on 5 May 2009 after a public competition won by Cristina Corradi de Beltran. The colours are those of the Argentine national flag; the central pehuen represents the province's iconic Araucaria araucana, while the red star and eight gold stars together honour the Mapuche-Pehuenche heritage and the geographic divisions of Neuquen.

features.symbolism: the celestial blue and white of the Argentine national flag
the monkey-puzzle tree, sacred to the Pehuenche people who give the province its name and its principal natural emblem
the eight regions of the province
the blood of the Mapuche-Pehuenche people and of the criollo settlers; the kultrun, a sacred Mapuche drum
